title: Jordan at FIFA World Cup 2026 — Ibrahim Sabra ruled out of tournament with injury
original_text: Jordan at FIFA World Cup 2026 — Ibrahim Sabra ruled out of tournament with injury left Scans revealed a tear in the ligaments of Ibrahim Sabra’s (centre) left ankle, ​sidelining him from ‌FIFA World Cup 2026. Getty Images Getty Images https://ss-i.thgim.com/public/incoming/namrf2/article71068709.ece/alternates/FREE_1200/GettyImages-2199929541.jpg Zhizhao Wu SHENZHEN, CHINA - FEBRUARY 16: Ibrahim Mohammad Sabra #7 of Jordan celebrates his second goal during the AFC U20 Asian Cup Group B between North Korea and Jordan at the Shenzhen Longhua Cultural and Sports Centre Stadium on February 16, 2025 in Shenzhen, China. (Photo by Zhizhao Wu/Getty Images) Ibrahim Sabra was one of the goalscoring options in Jordan’s squad, with the country playing in the FIFA World Cup for the first time, in the USA, Canada and Mexico. Jordan’s 20-year-old ​forward Ibrahim Sabra has been ‌ruled out of the World ​Cup in North ⁠America after suffering a torn ankle ligament in training, the ‌country’s football association (JFA) said on Friday. The JFA ‌confirmed in a statement ‌on ⁠X that scans had ⁠revealed a tear in the ligaments of Sabra’s left ankle, ​sidelining him from ‌the tournament starting next week. The Lokomotiv Zagreb striker has recently broken into the ‌senior national team after ​impressing at youth level. His absence is ⁠a blow to Jordan coach Jamal Al-Salami’s attacking options. Jordan is preparing for its debut World Cup following a highly successful period that included reaching the Asian Cup 2023 final. It has been drawn in Group J ​and will face Austria and Algeria in San ⁠Francisco, before playing reigning champion ⁠Argentina in Dallas.
